chapters.title: Grievance Redressal Mechanism
detailed_topics: How to register complaint on IGMS portal
Consumer, Defect, Complaint, Consumer Dispute — definitions
District Forum — jurisdiction up to Rs. 20 lakhs
State Commission — jurisdiction Rs. 20L to Rs. 1 Cr
National Commission — jurisdiction above Rs. 1 Cr
Procedure for filing complaint — no fee, no advocate needed
Consumer Forum Orders — 5 types of orders
Who can complain to Ombudsman & conditions
Time limit — within 1 year of insurer rejection
Ombudsman Award — max Rs. 30 lakh, within 3 months
Award binding on insurer — comply within 30 days

chapters.title: Regulatory Aspects of Insurance Agents
detailed_topics: Key Definitions — Agent, Composite Agent, Mono-line Insurer, Designated Official
Centralised List of Agents & Blacklisted Agents
Appointment of Insurance Agent — Form I-A process
Insurance Agency Examination — eligibility & pass certificate (valid 12 months)
Disqualification conditions — Section 42(3) of Insurance Act
Code of Conduct — 13 DO's for agents
Code of Conduct — 11 DON'Ts for agents
Penalty — Rs. 1 Crore for insurer violation
Resignation/Surrender — Cessation Certificate (Form I-C) within 15 days
90-day cooling period before joining new insurer
General conditions — max 1 Life + 1 General + 1 Health insurer

chapters.title: Legal Principles of an Insurance Contract
detailed_topics: Indian Contract Act 1872 — governs all insurance contracts
Elements of a Valid Contract — 6 elements
Consideration — premium vs promise to indemnify
Consensus Ad-Idem — agreement in same sense
Free Consent — Coercion, Undue Influence, Fraud, Misrepresentation, Mistake
Capacity of Parties — minors cannot enter insurance contracts
Uberrima Fides — Utmost Good Faith (most important principle)
Caveat Emptor vs Utmost Good Faith distinction
Material Information — what must be disclosed
Breach of Utmost Good Faith — policy voidable
Proximate Cause — nearest cause of loss
Contract of Adhesion — take it or leave it
Indemnity — restore to same position, not profit
Subrogation — insurer steps into insured's shoes
